title: argemone corymbosa var. arenicola
intro: Argemone corymbosa var. arenicola, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy or desert prickly poppy, is a variant of the Argemone corymbosa plant, a member of the Papaveraceae family, native to the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, making it a valuable herbal remedy in folk medicine. Argemone corymbosa var. arenicola is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its seeds are used to make a medicinal oil, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity.
